symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden well-demarcated round patches of non-scarring hair loss with active shedding

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Focal, sharply demarcated patchy loss is the hallmark pattern of alopecia areata.
Telogen effluvium is diffuse and uniform, not focal well-demarcated patches.

symptoms.symptom_name: Trichodynia — burning, painful scalp sensation during active shedding

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Trichodynia is a recognised benign accompaniment of active alopecia areata; it is non-specific and does NOT indicate infection or a destructive process.

symptoms.symptom_name: Smooth, normal-appearing underlying scalp skin — no scale, pustules, crusting, erythema or scarring

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of scaling, pustules, crusting and boggy swelling makes tinea capitis/kerion unlikely.
No erythema, follicular plugging or scale; definitive exclusion of loss of follicular ostia still needs in-person examination.
Non-scarring, non-inflamed patches with preserved follicular openings fit alopecia areata.

symptoms.symptom_name: Exclamation-mark hairs at the active patch margins on dermoscopy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exclamation-mark hairs (short hairs tapering at the base) are highly characteristic of active alopecia areata — a dermoscopic finding not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive hair-pull test at the active margins

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A strongly positive pull test at active margins confirms ongoing rapid shedding — a clinician-performed manoeuvre, sealed from telehealth.
Nail pitting reflects nail-matrix involvement and supports alopecia areata.
Alopecia areata clusters with other autoimmune disease; coexisting autoimmune thyroiditis raises its prior probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute anxiety/panic with hyperventilation, insomnia and catastrophic thinking

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden distressing hair loss precipitating a panic reaction is the secondary clinical problem requiring active management and safety-netting.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia, tachypnoea and mildly elevated blood pressure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sympathetic arousal from acute panic explains these clinician-measured observations; they do not indicate an organic emergency. Sealed clinician measurement — the AI Doctor infers arousal from the patient's reported palpitations and breathlessness, not a measured value.
Kerion/tinea capitis typically produces tender regional lymphadenopathy; its absence argues against an infective process.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together, focal patches on a smooth non-inflamed scalp with dermoscopic exclamation-mark hairs, a positive pull test, nail pitting and an autoimmune background make alopecia areata the clear diagnosis and exclude infective/scarring mimics.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person dermoscopy (trichoscopy) of the active margins at GP/dermatology review
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms exclamation-mark hairs and distinguishes alopecia areata from mimics; cannot be done via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Scalp scraping for fungal microscopy/culture (only if scaling/pustules appear)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated with a smooth, non-scaly, non-pustular scalp; would only apply if inflammatory/infective features emerged.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Thyroid function review with the usual GP
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Reasonable given known autoimmune thyroid disease and the autoimmune association, at routine follow-up — not urgent and not part of the acute telehealth encounter.
